title: GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
text:
GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov) - SupportGoWhere
GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov)
Share link
Equips fresh graduates with experience and skills, easing their transition into full-time employment.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
3 to 6-month traineeships in Public Service for fresh graduates
Enables fresh graduates to gain useful skills and experience through short-term traineeships
Trainees receive a monthly training allowance ranging from S$1,800 to $2,400
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

The GRaduate Industry Traineeships @Gov (GRIT@Gov) Programme supports fresh graduates in gaining experience and skills, to facilitate their transition into full-time employment. GRIT@Gov offers traineeships for fresh graduates within public sector agencies.

 

Trainees will receive a monthly training allowance ranging from S$1,800 to $2,400 for the duration of the traineeship. This training allowance may vary depending on the scope of each traineeship.

Who is this for?

All trainees must fulfil the following criteria:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident; and

Graduated or graduating in 2024, 2025 or 2026 from Universities, Polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education (ITE) and other Educational Institutions (e.g. private universities and overseas institutions); or

Graduated earlier from above institutions and completed National Service in 2024, 2025 or 2026. 

Applicants must have no prior working experience and must be prepared to commit to a full-time traineeship.

 

Trainees who complete their studies from Universities, Polytechnics, the Institute of Technical Education (ITE) or other Educational Institutions within 2026 but who will only receive their qualification/certification award in 2027 are eligible for the programme as well.

How to apply?

Browse and apply for GRIT@Gov traineeships via Careers@Gov.

 

If you are a fresh graduate who is considering a role in the Public Service, check out our Early Careers page.

 

To view all vacancies across the Public Service (including for mid-careerists), please visit our Careers@Gov job portal.

Where can I find help?

View more details 

Submit an enquiry
